
## Conditions (skin, interface, border, surface)

### Boundary Conditions

$\textbf{n} \cdot \left[ \mathscr{D} \right] = \sigma$

$\textbf{n} \cdot \left[ \mathscr{B} \right] = 0$

$\textbf{n} \times \left[ \mathscr{E} \right] = 0$

$\textbf{n} \times \left[ \mathscr{H} \right] = J$



### Interior Conditions
e.g. for Superconductivity

$\textbf{n} \cdot \mathscr{H} = 0$

$\textbf{n} \times \mathscr{D} = 0$


### Abreviations

$\textbf{n} =$ normal vector (orthogonality orientation)

$\mathscr{E} =$ elec. Field ("traction force"?)

$\mathscr{B} =$ magn. Field ("compressive force"?)

$\mathscr{D} =$ elec. Displacement Field ("exitioan")

$\mathscr{H} =$ magn. Exitation Field ("incentivation")

$\sigma =$ electric charge at surface

$J =$ current density at surface
